clientInit.explanation: S3Client is an interface with no public constructors but provides a static builder() method, which is the standard AWS SDK v2 pattern for creating configured client instances. The builder pattern allows developers to customize client settings like credentials, regions, and retry policies before instantiation.

connectionFields.name: httpClient
connectionFields.description: Custom HTTP client for making requests to S3, allowing control over connection pooling, timeouts, and other HTTP-level settings.

connectionFields.name: serviceConfiguration
connectionFields.description: S3-specific service configuration including path-style access, accelerate mode, dualstack, checksum validation, and multi-region settings.

connectionFields.name: endpointProvider
connectionFields.description: Provider that determines the service endpoint for each S3 request, allowing custom endpoint resolution logic.

connectionFields.name: authSchemeProvider
connectionFields.description: Provider that resolves the authentication scheme for each S3 request, supporting various AWS signing protocols.

connectionFields.name: accelerate
connectionFields.description: Enables S3 Transfer Acceleration endpoints for faster data transfers over long distances.

connectionFields.name: disableMultiRegionAccessPoints
connectionFields.description: Disables the use of Multi-Region Access Points for simplified global bucket access.

connectionFields.name: disableS3ExpressSessionAuth
connectionFields.description: Disables Session Auth for S3 Express One Zone storage and uses standard SigV4 authentication instead.

connectionFields.name: forcePathStyle
connectionFields.description: Forces path-style addressing for bucket access (e.g., s3.amazonaws.com/bucket) instead of virtual-hosted-style.

connectionFields.name: useArnRegion
connectionFields.description: Uses the region from the bucket ARN for endpoint construction instead of the client's configured region.

connectionFields.name: crossRegionAccessEnabled
connectionFields.description: Enables cross-region bucket access allowing the client to access buckets in different regions than configured.

connectionFields.name: sigv4aSigningRegionSet
connectionFields.description: Specifies the set of regions to use for SigV4a signing, enabling multi-region request signatures.

connectionFields.name: credentialsProvider
connectionFields.description: Provider for AWS credentials used to authenticate and sign requests to S3.

connectionFields.name: region
connectionFields.description: AWS region where the S3 service endpoint is located.

connectionFields.name: defaultsMode
connectionFields.description: Configuration defaults mode that optimizes client settings for different environments like mobile, cross-region, or standard usage.

connectionFields.name: dualstackEnabled
connectionFields.description: Enables dual-stack endpoints supporting both IPv4 and IPv6 addresses.

connectionFields.name: fipsEnabled
connectionFields.description: Enables FIPS-compliant endpoints for compliance with US government security standards.

connectionFields.name: endpointOverride
connectionFields.description: Custom endpoint URI to override the default S3 service endpoint.

connectionFields.name: putAuthScheme
connectionFields.description: Adds a custom authentication scheme to the client's auth scheme registry.

subFields.name: pathStyleAccessEnabled
subFields.description: Enable path-style bucket access instead of virtual-hosted-style.

subFields.name: accelerateModeEnabled
subFields.description: Enable S3 Transfer Acceleration for faster uploads.

subFields.name: dualstackEnabled
subFields.description: Enable IPv4 and IPv6 dual-stack endpoints.

subFields.name: checksumValidationEnabled
subFields.description: Enable automatic checksum validation for S3 operations.

subFields.name: accessKeyId
subFields.description: AWS access key ID for authentication.

subFields.name: secretAccessKey
subFields.description: AWS secret access key for authentication.

subFields.name: sessionToken
subFields.description: Optional session token for temporary credentials.
